message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: My task: fix IDCG calculation in NDCG metric to use expected relevant docs count instead of retrieved docs count. Only touches metrics.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: My task: add optional relevance_scores dict param to NDCG.compute() in metrics.py for graded relevance. Need to edit this file. If your feature touches a different file, great - fully disjoint. If your feature also needs metrics.py, let's discuss which class/function each of us touches so we don't clobber.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both must touch NDCG.compute() in the same file. Proposal to avoid clobbering: my fix is tiny and surgical - only the 'Calculate IDCG' block (currently 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' plus the idcg sum), changing it to iterate over len(expected_ids) only. I will NOT touch the DCG calc, docstring, or signature. I'll make this edit first and send DONE quickly (should take <2 min). Please wait for my DONE before editing metrics.py, then build your relevance_scores param on top of my already-fixed idcg block. This avoids us stomping each other's concurrent writes to the same file. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both need NDCG.compute() in the same file - accepting shared ownership since it's unavoidable. Our changes overlap on the idcg calculation block (lines ~380-386). To compose cleanly: I'll implement relevance_scores support (dict mapping expected_id->float, default 1.0) used in BOTH dcg and idcg loops, and I'll incorporate your ideal_length fix (using len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))) as part of my edit so the two features compose correctly rather than clobbering. Let me know if you disagree with that ideal_length formula before I proceed - otherwise I'll start implementing now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Agreed. I'll wait for your DONE before touching metrics.py, then build relevance_scores on top of your fixed idcg block.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Agreed on the formula (len(expected_ids) only, dropping the min()). One correction: each of us needs to independently submit our own patch.txt for our own assigned feature, so I do need to make this specific edit myself (not have you fold it into yours) - otherwise my submission has no diff. I'm making the tiny idcg edit right now and will send DONE within a minute. Please hold off editing metrics.py until you see my DONE, then layer relevance_scores on top of the already-fixed idcg block. Thanks for checking first.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Understood - go ahead and make your idcg edit yourself. I'll hold off touching metrics.py until I see your DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Done. Edited only the IDCG block: removed 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' and changed the idcg sum's range to 'range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)' directly. Nothing else in the file touched (DCG calc, docstring, signature all untouched). Verified with a quick manual test - e.g. expected_ids with 4 items but only 2 retrieved now correctly yields ndcg < 1.0 instead of the old inflated 1.0. metrics.py is free for you to layer relevance_scores on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Done. Added optional relevance_scores: Optional[Dict[str, float]] param to NDCG.compute(). Kept the existing ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) formula unchanged since I'm not overlapping into your idcg-cap fix (we're in separate repo copies so there was nothing to merge). DCG now uses relevance(docid) instead of binary docid in expected_set; IDCG now sorts graded relevance scores of expected docs descending and takes the top ideal_length. Binary behavior fully preserved when relevance_scores is omitted. All 42 existing tests in test_metrics.py pass. Writing patch.txt now.
